name: Rekha — Widow who remarried
description: Tests whether engine correctly updates eligibility when marital status changes. Widow pension schemes should stop, but other schemes should remain.
what_should_break: Engine might still show widow pension eligibility after remarriage, or might incorrectly remove schemes that don't depend on marital status.

name: Ramesh — Farmer who leases land
description: Tests land ownership vs land access. PM Kisan requires ownership, MGNREGA doesn't care. Many schemes say 'farmer' without clarifying if tenant farmers count.
what_should_break: Engine might mark PM Kisan as eligible because occupation=farmer, ignoring that land_ownership=false.

name: Priya — Has Aadhaar but no bank account
description: Tests the dependency chain. Many DBT schemes require both Aadhaar AND bank account.
what_should_break: Engine might show full eligibility for PM Kisan or Ujjwala despite no bank account, since payment can't be processed without one.

name: Kavitha — Transgender person
description: Tests gender handling. Many schemes use binary male/female categories.
what_should_break: Engine might either exclude from gender-specific schemes they're eligible for, or confidently include in schemes that haven't been updated for transgender inclusion.

name: Mohammad — Inter-state migrant worker
description: Tests domicile vs working state. From Bihar but works in Maharashtra.
what_should_break: Engine might apply Bihar scheme rules while the user lives in Maharashtra, or might not know which state's income thresholds to use.

name: Sharma Family — Joint family with pooled income
description: Tests income calculation. Joint family total is 8L but individual is 1.5L.
what_should_break: Engine might use individual income and wrongly qualify for BPL schemes, or use family income and wrongly exclude from individual-eligibility schemes.

name: Lakshmi — Just above BPL cutoff
description: Tests boundary conditions. Income 2.55L when Ayushman cutoff is ~2.5L.
what_should_break: Engine might give confident 'not eligible' for a 2% difference, when SECC listing is what actually matters.

name: Arjun — Minor orphan (age 16)
description: Tests age boundaries and guardian requirements.
what_should_break: Engine might reject everything because age < 18, but some schemes specifically cover minors.

name: Sunita — Disabled, certificate from different state
description: Tests disability benefit portability across states.
what_should_break: Engine might confidently show disability scheme eligibility without flagging certificate re-verification need.

name: Raju — Unclear occupation (auto driver)
description: Tests occupation ambiguity. Auto-rickshaw driver doesn't map cleanly to scheme categories.
what_should_break: Engine might not match unorganized worker schemes if occupation doesn't map cleanly to any category.

name: Govind — Happy path: PM Vishwakarma artisan
description: Clean match for PM Vishwakarma. Traditional blacksmith, all documents in order. Should score near 1.0 confidence.
expected_result: PM Vishwakarma: fully eligible. Also eligible for Atal Pension, PMSBY, PMJJBY, Jan Dhan (already has account). Should NOT match PM Kisan (not a farmer).

name: Meena — Happy path: Rural BPL woman
description: Clean match for maximum schemes. Rural, SC, SECC-listed, low income, female. Should qualify for Ayushman Bharat, MGNREGA, Ujjwala, PMAY-G, Matru Vandana, Jan Dhan.
expected_result: Should match 8+ schemes with high confidence. This is the 'ideal beneficiary' the government designed these schemes for.

name: Irfan — Happy path: PM VIKAS minority entrepreneur
description: Clean match for PM VIKAS (minority welfare). Muslim small business owner, urban, low income.
expected_result: PM VIKAS: fully eligible. Also Mudra Yojana (small business loan), Atal Pension, PMSBY, PMJJBY. Should NOT match MGNREGA (urban) or PM Kisan (not farmer).

name: Devi — Happy path: Small farmer, perfect PM Kisan match
description: Clean match for PM Kisan. Owns 1.2ha, not in exclusion list, all docs ready.
expected_result: PM Kisan: fully eligible (confidence ~1.0). Also MGNREGA, Fasal Bima, Atal Pension, PMSBY. PM Kisan exclusion checks should all pass (not govt employee, not income tax payer, not pensioner).

name: Kamala — Happy path: Ayushman Bharat SECC beneficiary
description: Clean match for Ayushman Bharat. SECC-listed, no vehicle, not EPF member. Tests that secc_listed field directly resolves AB eligibility without income guessing.
expected_result: Ayushman Bharat: fully eligible (confidence ~1.0). Also MGNREGA, Ujjwala, PMAY-G. The secc_listed=true should be the primary eligibility signal, not income thresholds.
